folio.label: 159v
folio.l: But if thow prike out of myn haunt ,,
A noon I slee thyn steede ,,
Witú Mace
Heere is the Quene of fairye
Witú harpe & pipe & Symphonye
Dwellyngö in this place
The child seide also mot I the
To morwe wol I mete the
Whan I haue myn Armoure
And ©et I hope ¹ ma fay
That æè shalt w this launcegay
Abyen it ful soure
Thy Mawe
I shal perce if I may
Or it be fully péme of day
For heere thow shalt be slawe
Sire Thopas drow a bak ful faste
This Geau~t at hi~ stones caste
Out of a fel staf slynge
But faire a skapitú child Thopas
And al it was thurgú goddes gras
And thurgú his fair berynge
ñet listenetú lordes to my tale
Meriere than the Nygútyngale
I wol ©ow rowne
How Sire Thopas witú sydes smale
Prykyngö ouä hil and dale
Is come a geyn to towne
His merie men comaunded he
To make hi~ bothe game & gle
For nedes must he figúte
Witú a Geau~t witú hedes thre
For paramour and Iolite
Of on that shon ful brigúte
Do come he seide my Mynestrales
And gestoures for to telle tales
A non in myn Armyngö
Of romaunces that ben reals
Of Popes and of Cardynales
And eke of loue longyngö
They fette hi~ first swete wyn
And Mede eke in a Maselyn
And real spicerie
Of gyngäbred that was ful fyn
And lycoris and eke Comyn
Witú sugre æ is trie
He dede next his white ler
Of clotú of lake fyn & cler
A breche & eke a sherte
And next his shert an Aketou~
And ouä that an haberiou~
For percyngö of his herte
And ouä that a fyn hauberk
Was al I wrougút of Iewes werk
Ful strongö it was of plate
And ouä that his cote armour
As white as is a lilie flour
In whicú he wol debate
His sheld was al of gold so red
And æäe Inne was a Bores hed
A charbocle be his syde
And æäe he swor on ale & bred
How that the Geau~t shal be ded
Bityde what bytyde
Hise Iambeus were of quyrbuly
His swerd shede of Yuory
Helme of latou~ brigút
